div.head: 概述
div.p: 《大般涅槃經》（ Mahā-pari-nirvāṇa-sūtra ，以下簡稱《涅槃經》）是卧佛院所有刻經中最長的一部，其刊刻始於59號窟。爲彰顯此重大工程，窟壁上刻經面周圍特别雕出裝飾帶。《涅槃經》第一卷刊刻於d壁（），第二、三卷刻滿整個e壁并延續至f壁，但省略了第三卷末尾較短的一品，直接開始刊刻第四卷。第四卷完成後，《涅槃經》的刊刻告一段落。取而代之的是節取自兩部佛經的禪法儀則，唐開元二十三年（735）由俗人弟子李涉施刻。其後是清信女滿□施刻的篇幅較短的《佛説報父母恩重經》。
59號窟是少數幾座窟口外刻有經文的洞窟之一。a壁再次刻出《佛説報父母恩重經》，其刊刻年代極有可能晚於窟内。

div.head: 位置

list.item: 位於南崖，D區，高於現今步道約415厘米，步道自西向東緩緩下傾。
f壁方向：314˚。
緊鄰C區三連式洞窟中的58號窟。59號窟窟内地面較58號窟低36厘米左右。

div.p: a壁刻經面上方有一尊立像（60號）。窟口另一側亦有一尊，編號（60-1）。59號窟西側爲61號龕，其内雕刻常見的一鋪七尊式造像組合，即居中一尊坐佛，兩側脅侍二弟子、二菩薩和二力士，位於29號窟和33號窟之間的31號龕亦屬此類（《中國佛教石經·四川省》第二卷第5頁， 圖3，和第6頁，圖4）。洞窟東側是更顯眼的62號龕，同樣雕刻一鋪七尊像，不過龕座三足香爐兩側各有一對供養人像。（）

div.head: 形制
div.p: 敞口方形窟。平面近方形，向内漸寬。平頂，窟内地面和頂部皆向内逐漸擡升。
與C區三連窟不同，59號窟外無石階通向窟内（）。窟口下方有一塊巨大的、向上傾斜的突出岩體。最初可能是經由木質樓梯或梯子進入該窟，如今祗能從58號窟穿過一道門進入。這道門是20世紀60年代在窟内圈養牲畜時所開，破壞了59號窟d壁（同時也是58號窟f壁，）。

list.item: a壁：高128厘米，寬36－46厘米。
d壁：前端高239厘米、後端高252厘米，寬251厘米。
e壁：中部高249厘米，寬257厘米。
f壁：前端高250厘米、後端高247厘米，寬279厘米。
窟口：寬208厘米。

div.head: 改造
div.p: 最大的改造爲 58號窟和59號窟之間的門道（）。門檻和門楣分别高出59號窟地面32厘米和210厘米。門底部寬56厘米，頂部寬58厘米。窟内近窟口處的地面上鑿有一道凹槽，用於排水。

list.item: 打磨面尺寸爲126×36－46厘米。
刻經面尺寸爲113.5×33厘米，下緣距59號窟窟内地面13厘米。刻文略微延伸至相鄰的61號龕南壁（），因而年代應晚於61號龕。

div.head: 保存狀况
div.p: 第16行下部無存。刻經區右上角覆蓋有黑斑。

row.cell: 佛說報父母恩重經；標識《佛説報父母恩重經》的開始。
報父母恩重經；標識《佛説報父母恩重經》的結束；此處經名省略“佛說”字。

div.head: 佈局

list.item: 裝飾邊帶

div.p: 上邊帶：高5－17厘米，兩身飛天雲氣繚繞，右者往左飛翔，左者回首右側，縱身自上空飛下。
北邊帶：寬6－7厘米，飾以花卉及小坐佛像的卷草紋。
南邊帶：寬16－17厘米，被現代開鑿的門打破，卷草紋裝飾帶僅存最上部和最下部。
下邊帶：高20厘米，七扇内雕神獸的壼門。

list.item: 打磨面與形狀爲不規則四邊形的刻經區一致。前端高192厘米，後端高216厘米，但在20世紀所開門楣上方僅餘21厘米高。寬226厘米，下部由於門道破壞，寬186厘米。上緣向窟内逐步升高：第29至68行刻經較前28行高出一横排，第69行較第29至68行高出一横排，第70至76行較第69行高出兩排，第77行至最後的第107行較其前又高出一排。下緣大體水平，前端距窟内地面29厘米，後端距地面21厘米。
刻經面在書寫風格上也可劃分爲四部分：

div.p: 第76（自第二卷經名始）－107行。

figure.head: 59號窟平面圖。雷魯卡根據馬本漢和王平國田野繪圖製作
Ground plan of cave 59; drawing by Lukas Ledderose after field map by MB and MS

div.head: 保存狀况
div.p: 大體完好。壁面不甚平整。近窟口、距窟内地面149－163厘米高處，有長方形人工鑿孔，加上其外鑿痕，破壞了裝飾邊框和第1－9行部分經文。
距地面148－151厘米高處，有一排細窄的横向自然裂隙，自第45行經文延伸至第71行；它們在經文刊刻之前已存在，工匠將其納入排版考量。另有一排窄裂隙在地面上方約140厘米處，自第67行經文延伸至第89行。
一帶狀剥蝕區域貫穿整個壁面，其前端距地面46－53厘米，後端距地面25－47厘米。中部壁面距地面約32－102厘米處被黑色污斑覆蓋，自窟口内大約80厘米處開始，一直延伸至後開鑿的門道。距窟口約100－164厘米、地面上方約100－205厘米的區域内可見墨書塗鴉。最後19行經文在開鑿門道時被鑿去，僅存留一殘塊。
一道裂縫自窟頂後端延伸至外側飛天上方。

row.cell: 大般涅槃經壽命品第一；標識《大般涅槃經》第一卷“壽命品”第一之一的開始。
大般涅槃經卷第一；標識《大般涅槃經》第一卷的結束，即“壽命品”第一之一的結束。
大般涅槃經壽命品之二； 其後刻小字“二”；標識《大般涅槃經》“壽命品”第一之二的開始，即《大般涅槃經》第二卷的開始。小字“二”標識《大般涅槃經》第二卷的開始。
衆生皆；d壁最末三字，“衆”字上半部毁損。

figure.head: 59號窟f壁門框凹槽。2010年拍攝
Incision for a door fitting on wall f in cave 59; photograph taken in 2010

row.cell: 第1－28行；之後隱約可見縱格綫痕迹
第1－28行：0.4－1.1厘米；第29－68行：約0.2厘米；第69－76行：0.1－0.9厘米，是四部分中最密且最不規則的，有行書元素，止於第76行第一卷經文結尾處；第76－107行：0.1－0.5厘米，自第二卷經名開始，佈局再次變規整

div.head: 佈局

list.item: 裝飾邊帶

div.p: 上邊帶：高15.5－18厘米，兩身飛天自雲氣繚繞，手托鉢，往中央對稱花束飛去。
西邊帶：寬10厘米，飾以花卉及小坐佛像的卷草紋。
東邊帶：寬14厘米，飾以花卉及小坐佛像的卷草紋。
下邊帶：高20厘米，六扇壼門，其内相間雕出神獸和伎樂。

list.item: 打磨面分别超出東、西兩側的裝飾邊帶約3.5－8.5厘米和 2.0－2.5厘米。
裝飾邊帶内的矩形刻經區寬226厘米；西側高207厘米，東側高208厘米。上緣大致水平；下緣不規則，東、西兩側分别距窟内地面20厘米和25厘米。

div.head: 保存狀况
div.p: 大體完好。壁面相當平整。岩體運動造成左上角的一塊三角形區域向前推移，一道自窟頂後端（岩石呈層狀剥落）延伸下來的裂隙將其與壁面主體分隔開來。裂隙繼續延伸至裝飾邊帶，在地面上方約88厘米處逐漸消失。裂隙現已填補水泥。另有一道縱向裂隙，沿e壁與f壁相接轉角處一直延伸下來，呈對角綫穿過地面。該裂隙亦填充了水泥。這些裂縫均爲山體内部力量作用的結果，并且與相鄰66號窟的一道裂隙連通。66號窟的這條裂隙貫穿整個d壁，并穿過窟頂延伸至f壁以及地面。
距地面約128－148厘米高處，有一條摻雜管狀結晶黏土的横向裂隙，始於e壁與d壁轉角處，在e壁延伸長度約爲157厘米，導致第1－72行經文所在壁面有若干狹縫和孔隙。地面上方78－87厘米處，有一矩形人工鑿孔，破壞了第31－34行刻經。另一處矩形人工鑿孔，距地面145－156厘米，破壞了第30－35行部分刻經。
東側壁面距地面上方約14－145厘米區域表面呈黑色。

row.cell: 左部區域隱約可見縱格綫痕迹
悉悦可纯陁……；延續d壁經文。
大般涅槃經卷第二；標識《大般涅槃經》第二卷的結束，即“壽命品”第一之二的結束。
大般涅槃經卷第三；標識《大般涅槃經》第三卷的開始，即“壽命品”第一之三，也是該品最後一部分的開始。
大般涅槃經金剛身品第二；標識《大般涅槃經》第三卷中“金剛身品”第二的開始。
然後乃扵娑羅；e壁末尾幾字。

div.head: 佈局

list.item: 裝飾邊帶

div.p: 上邊帶：前端高10厘米，後端高18厘米，兩身飛天雲氣繚繞，向位於中央對稱花束飛去，右側飛天手托鉢。
南邊帶：寬8厘米，飾以花卉及小坐佛像的卷草紋。
北邊帶：幾乎完全毁壞。
下邊帶：高20厘米，六扇壼門，多風化殘損。

list.item: 打磨面在後端超出裝飾邊框3.5－5.0厘米。
刻經區形狀近平行四邊形。下緣不規則，向洞窟後端呈階梯狀逐步升高，上緣則平滑擡升。刻經區寬249厘米；前端高202厘米，後端高213厘米；前、後端分别距地面25厘米和18厘米。

div.head: 保存狀况
div.p: 大體完好。壁面不甚平整；地面上方約152－159厘米處有一條横向裂隙，在第12－93行刻經所在壁面形成若干管狀結晶黏土。地面上方82－87厘米處，有一人工開鑿的小孔洞，破壞了第3－5行的部分刻經。有一用於安裝門框的凹槽，尺寸爲157×8厘米，自地面延伸至第93－96行刻經（）。地面上方161.5－171厘米處有一圓形鑿孔，與d壁的類似孔洞位置對應，破壞了第92－98行的部分刻經；另一圓孔，距地面158－161厘米高，鑿入第110－111行經文。還有一人工鑿孔，距地面231－238厘米，位於第99－103行經文上方，未對刻經區造成影響。
門框凹槽與窟口之間的壁面風化剥落。

row.cell: 雙樹入般涅槃……；延續e壁經文。
大般涅槃經卷第三；標識《大般涅槃經》第三卷的結束，即第二品“金剛身”的結束。該經第三卷中，第二品後有“名字功德品”第三。然而，該品未被刊刻，刻經直接跳至第四卷第四品。
大般涅槃經如来性品第四；標識《大般涅槃經》第四卷的開始，即“如來性品”第四之一的開始。
大般涅槃經卷第四；標識《大般涅槃經》第四卷的結束，即“如來性品”第四之一的結束。
大乘大集地藏十輪經卷第三；標識其後節文的第一出處《大乘大集地藏十輪經》（下稱《十輪經》）第三卷。
佛說禪法略出；標識下文更常出現的經名《禪秘要經》，這是其後節文的第二出處。 該經的不同經名見上文，。
佛言大梵有十種……；標識《十輪經》節録經文的開始。
智者應供養；《十輪經》節録經文結尾之字。
禪秘要經一部三卷；標識一部三卷本《禪秘要經》。
已上出十輪經第三卷；標識《十輪經》第三卷節録經文的結束。
第二卷；標識《禪秘要經》第二卷爲其後十二段節文的出處。
略出初學坐禪法流通末代得者頂戴受持……；陳述意圖，即該經受眾及使用方法。
佛告阿難汝好受持；“佛”前空格。標識《禪秘要經》第一段節文的開始。
佛告阿難善男子復；“佛”前空格。標識《禪秘要經》第二段節文的開始。
又今我身内自有四；“又”前空格。標識《禪秘要經》第三段節文的開始。
佛告阿難此不浄觀；“佛”前空格。標識《禪秘要經》第四段節文的開始。
“得生天上”後和“若復有人”前空格。
佛說法者說四念處；“佛”前空格。標識《禪秘要經》第五段節文的開始。
千二百五十比丘皆。標識《禪秘要經》第六段節文的開始。
佛告阿難貪淫多者；“佛”前空格。標識《禪秘要經》第七段節文的開始。
“貪淫多者”與“復當系念注意”之間省略45字。
“往反至十”與“或單或複”之間省略202字。
或單或複修長短随意。標識《禪秘要經》第八段節文的開始。
佛告阿難此不浄觀；“佛”前空格。標識《禪秘要經》第九段節文的開始。
佛告言法子汝今應；“佛”前空格。標識《禪秘要經》第十段節文的開始。
佛告阿難若有比丘；“佛”前空格。標識《禪秘要經》第十一段節文的開始。
歡喜奉行。標識《禪秘要經》第十一段節文的結束。
禪法秘要第二卷；“禪”前空格。標識《禪法秘要》（前稱《禪秘要經》）第二卷，以上和以下節文皆出自其中。
如是我聞；標識《禪秘要經》卷二第十二段節文的開始，即組成《禪秘要經》的四部獨立佛經中第三部佛經的開始。
“敷尼師檀結跏趺坐”後和“尔時眾中有一比丘名”前空格。
“說止觀法”後和“佛告槃直迦汝從今日”前空格。
“端坐繫心”後和“佛告槃直迦汝今當”前空格。
亦如上說；標識《禪秘要經》第二卷第十二段節文的結束。
禪經卷第三；標識《禪經》（前稱《禪秘要經》）第三卷，以下三段經文即節録自其中。
佛告阿難；“佛”前空格。標識《禪經》第三卷第一段節文的開始。
“必墮惡道犯大重罪”後和“若有比丘尼妖冶邪媚”前空格。
“必墮惡道犯大重罪”後和“若有比丘比丘尼實不見白骨”前空格。
［不可具說］，殘損；標識《禪經》第三卷第一段節文的結束。
［佛告阿難］，殘損；標識《禪經》第三卷第二段節文的開始。
悟觧脱道；標識《禪經》第三卷第二段節文的結束。
若有比丘比……；“若”前空格。標識《禪經》第三卷第三段節文的開始。
［佛］說禪祕要經；標識《佛説禪秘要經》所有節録經文的結束：十二段節文出自第二卷，三段出自第三卷。
開元廿三年二月十五日長江縣李涉敬造供養； 此735年題記當屬出自前面兩部佛經的禪法儀則。
佛說報父母恩重經；標識《佛説報父母恩重經》的開始。
［報父母恩重經］；標識《佛説報父母恩重經》的結束。
f壁最末行：……年六月卄六日清信女滿□供［養］；《佛説報父母恩重經》末尾題記，僅屬該經。

div.head: 討論
div.bibl:
胡文和及李官智第21頁
作者給出了經名，并認爲該窟飛天與廣元皇澤寺7號龕以及龍門和鞏縣石窟的飛天形像基本相同。

彭家勝第6－7、11頁
彭家勝記録了洞窟的測量尺寸，并計算出其體積爲16.302立方米。他辨識出d、e和f壁所刻《大般涅槃經》的所有卷品。他還指出節録自《大乘大集地藏十輪經》第三卷的經文，不過將其副標題“佛説禪法略出”作爲單獨的佛經列出。他亦列出了f壁的《禪秘要經》，以及a壁和f壁重複刊刻的兩部《佛説報父母恩重經》。此外，他迻録窟中的兩則題記，供養人姓名分别録爲“李沙”和“滿月”。

何恩之第41－42頁
見本卷66號窟“討論”。

曹丹第51頁
曹丹提及該窟刊刻的兩則題記，一則録爲唐開元廿三年（735）李沙供養題記，另一則録爲清信女滿□供養題記。

胡文和第7、9頁
胡文和給出了洞窟的三維尺寸（同彭家勝第6－7頁），但未計算其體積。他採用了彭家勝提供的刻文位置，但略去或漏掉了a壁的《佛説報父母恩重經》。他也迻録了f壁的兩則題記。此外，還簡要介紹了《佛説報父母恩重經》的版本流傳。他辨識供養人名字爲“李涉”與“滿□”。

鄧之金第36頁
鄧之金稱本窟各壁面刻滿佛經，但僅列舉f壁刻經的卷品名。他亦轉録f壁題記，將供養人姓名識讀爲“李沙”和“滿相”。

胡文和第64、67頁
胡文和重申上述胡文和第7、9頁的内容。

孫修身第239－249頁
孫修身1995年考察卧佛院時，得知59號窟f壁刊刻着《佛説報父母恩重經》。在迻録經文之前，他首先介紹該經的不同版本：
甲本，提及中國孝子典範，因此被《開元釋教録》列在《疑惑再譯録》中。
乙本，現存大部分敦煌遺書屬於該版本，未提及中國孝子典範。收録於《大正藏》第85册T#2887。
丙本，亦未提及中國孝子典範，將父母恩德歸納爲十種，且每種恩德均帶有小標題。該版本還詳盡描述子女因不行孝道在地獄受懲處的情狀。
丁本，僅見於敦煌17號窟藏經洞出土絹畫；被認爲可能是乙本的“删節本”，爲適應版面需要。儘管經名擴充爲《佛説報父母恩重經》，但是内容極爲簡短。
以上四種版本，孫修身均重新作校勘，并刊佈全文。他還提到由牧田諦亮（Makita Tairyō）最先述及的高麗本。而後孫修身根據甲本和乙本相應段落，補校并刊佈59號窟f壁刻經録文。他認同胡文和第291頁，59號窟刻經紀年爲開元二十三年（735）的説法，由此稱安岳本爲現存最早的一種版本，是甲本和乙本之間的過渡性版本。
孫修身未提及59號窟a壁刊刻的《佛説報父母恩重經》，儘管該壁刻文較f壁保存更爲完好。

李良和鄧之金第42頁
作者給出洞窟測量尺寸，列出各壁經文行數，計算刻經字數，識别了所有經品，分析裝飾邊帶雕刻圖案，并且將該窟年代定爲唐代。作者辨識供養人名爲“李沙”和“滿相”。

新井慧誉第105－111頁
新井慧誉爲真言宗僧人，壽德寺方丈，於東京大學取得博士學位，論文研究《報父母恩重經》。1997年他至卧佛院拍攝并勘定録文。根據“古本”，新井補校59號窟《佛説報父母恩重經》兩本刻本，就此重新確立一個新文本。因該版本在古本《父母恩重經》經名前面增加了“報”字，故稱之爲“報本”。

鄭阿財第30－33、35－36頁
鄭阿財列出他所知《父母恩重經》敦煌寫本共計60件，并將其歸爲四種版本：
甲本，又稱丁蘭本，約978字。該本述及包括丁蘭在内的三位中國孝子典範。該版内容與《開元釋教録》所載相合。
乙本，亦稱古本，約875字，與甲本相近，但略去103字孝子典範相關篇幅。
丙本，與乙本相近，但包含遵循盂蘭盆教義的訓誡。
丁本，此本爲擴寫本，其中有對父母十恩德以及十八地獄給予不孝行爲懲處的陳述。此版本與講經文、經變圖像緊密相關。
鄭阿財列出與各版本相符的現存敦煌寫本，并引録經文。當述及《父母恩重經》的石刻本時，鄭提到卧佛院59號窟f壁的石刻經。由於鄭阿財參考胡文和的研究，而後者忽略了保存狀况較好的a壁石刻經，因此，鄭着重强調f壁石刻本爲該經的最早版本，并將其歸入乙本。此文再版刊於鄭阿財第1－25頁。

成都考古發現第376－377、406頁
成都市文物考古研究所的編者提供了洞窟建築形制方面的詳細記録，包括所有壁面的測量尺寸。同時詳盡地描述了淺浮雕裝飾帶。他們在表格中給出各卷刻經壁面所處行數，註明《大正藏》中對應經本，并且迻録了題記。

曹丹和于春第12頁
曹丹和于春測量了各壁面尺寸與面積，并基於拓片給出各壁現存字數：d壁9844字、e壁12971字、f壁13552字。他們辨識出該窟刊刻的《涅槃經》卷品，亦提及“大乘大集地藏十輪經卷第三”“禪經卷第三”名稱，但忽略了該窟《佛説報父母恩重經》兩本刻文。最後，他們給出f壁二題記録文。

李琛妍第50－52、54－55和65－66頁
李琛妍將59號窟歸入她所劃分的第III組窟群，即本叢書C區。除窟外a壁的《佛説報父母恩重經》外，她列出了該窟刊刻的所有佛經。她認爲最初即未計劃刻出整部《大般涅槃經》，而是有選擇性地刊刻。59號窟和66號窟主要爲該經設計，而46號窟和51號窟的後續卷品，或爲後來補刻。她未注意到83號窟d壁刊刻有《大般涅槃經》的另外一段經文，因而稱該經僅限於她劃定的第III、IV組窟群中。
李琛妍還提出59號窟的兩部禪經節文與三階教有關聯。在論及陝西省金川灣刻經時，她注意到兩處刻經址均刊刻了《法華經》《金剛經》和《十輪經》，儘管金川灣所刻《十輪經》乃北凉舊譯本（T#410），而卧佛院則爲玄奘修訂譯本（T#411）。

秦臻、張雪芬和雷玉華第76－82、122－123和311－350頁
作者詳細描述了洞窟位置、建築形制及浮雕裝飾。壁面實測圖標示了經卷位置與殘損區域。文内亦抄録了題記及全部經文，并指出刻文與《大正藏》版本之間的差異，但刻文未標句讀。

鄧星亮第75－76、86－87、89－93、99－100、108、146－161、176－177頁
鄧星亮概述了59號窟，描述其裝飾邊帶，并給出部分測量數據。他迻録了開元二十三年（735）年題記及f壁《佛説報父母恩重經》的題記，供養人姓名分别各録爲“李沙”和“滿相”。由於兩方題記位置相近，鄧因此推測第二方題記的已毁紀年亦爲開元二十三年（735）。
鄧隨後列出刻於d壁、e及f壁的《大般涅槃經》所有卷品。他標註各卷、品始末行數，引出刻於各卷、品始末的相關文字。此外，他將卧佛院所刊刻《大般涅槃經》與北本、南本對應品名卷數列表比較，由此得出結論，認爲石刻本節取了北本第一至第十四卷内容，但進行了重新分卷。
他繼而記録刻於f壁的《大乘大集地藏十輪經》以及《禪祕要經》皆爲節選而成。他迻録了兩部刻經所刻經卷名，標識其順序，記録每處經卷名開頭與結尾的文段。然而他并未具體分析有多少處節文刊刻於此，哪些部分省略未刻。
鄧其後討論f壁所刻最後一部佛經，即共計9行的《佛説報父母恩重經》。他首先迻録了該經所帶題記，并提到新井慧誉所發現并迻録的刻於a壁的共計16行的《佛説報父母恩重經》。在討論《父母恩重經》各個版本時，鄧採用鄭阿財定義的四個主要系統（第30－33頁）。
基於新井慧誉之作（），鄧迻録了a壁以及f壁可辨識的經文。鄧亦指出新井慧誉經文復原不足之處。他認爲新井慧誉的“報本”以“古本”校補而成，并不適當。實際上，丙本更爲適合。他進一步認爲卧佛院刻本有其自成的版本，原因有三：
雖然刻經與乙本和丙本相近，a壁第15行下方佚失的經文并不能由此兩本中任何一本補充，因爲此兩種版本相較石刻本皆過長。
儘管刻經屬於《父母恩重經》類，然其標題爲《佛説報父母恩重經》，是獨一無二的。此標題未見任何藏經目録。卧佛院石刻本爲已知最早版本，應流通於59號窟題記紀年開元二十三年（735）之前。因而此經應撰成於735年之前。
道教典籍中有同名經文，即《太上老君説報父母恩重經》。秋月觀暎研究認爲此爲《佛説報父母恩重經》的原型（秋月觀暎）。由於卧佛院本紀年更早，鄧認爲，《佛説報父母恩重經》應爲道教典籍的原型，而非反之。
卧佛院版本有些表達與詞句略有出入，反映了經本在流傳過程中的變化。
爲表明其變化情况，鄧還提供卧佛院石刻本與甲本、乙本、丁本以及宋輯佚本的異文對照表。

div.head: Overview
div.p: In this cave the monks began their work of engraving the longest of all sutras in the Grove of the Reclining Buddha, the Great Parinirvāṇa Sutra ( Mahāparinirvāṇa-sūtra , Da banniepan jing 大般涅槃經; hereafter: Nirvana Sutra). They celebrated this important task by framing the fields of script with decorative borders. They embarked on this enterprise with scroll 1 of the sutra on wall d (fig.). The text continues with scrolls 2 and 3 across the entire wall e into wall f. There, however, the engravers omitted the last short chapter of scroll 3 and proceeded immediately to scroll 4. Having completed this scroll, they ceased to work on the Nirvana Sutra in this cave. In its place, instructions on meditation taken from two sutras were donated in 735 by the layman Li She 李涉. Thereafter follows the short Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents (Fo shuo bao fumu enzhong jing 佛説報父母恩重經) given by the laywomen Man [. . .] 滿□.

Cave 59 is one of only a handful of caves with engravings outside, alongside the opening. Here on wall a, once again is the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents. It was most likely carved later than the texts inside the cave.

div.head: Location

list.item: Southern slope of the valley, section D, ca. 415 cm above the present walkway, which descends slowly from west to east.
Direction: wall f 314˚.
Immediately adjacent to cave 58 of the tripartite group of caves in section C; the floor of cave 59 is 36 cm lower than that of cave 58.

figure.head: 59號窟平面圖。雷魯卡根據馬本漢和王平國田野繪圖製作
Ground plan of cave 59; drawing by Lukas Ledderose after field map by MB and MS

div.p: Above the field of script on wall a is a standing figure (60). On the other side of the opening of cave 59 is another standing figure (60-1). To the west is niche 61, filled with the usual symmetrical group of seven figures, namely a seated Buddha, flanked by two disciples, two bodhisattvas, and two guardians, as, for example, also seen in niche 31 between caves 29 and 33 (p. 5, fig. 3, and p. 6, fig. 4). To the east follows the more prominent niche 62, again with the group of seven figures, but below pairs of donors stand either side of an incense burner in the shape of a tripod (fig.).

div.head: Shape
div.p: Box shape with open front. The ground plan is approximately square, but widens toward the rear; the floor and the horizontal ceiling rise toward the rear.

Unlike the three neighboring caves of section C, cave 59 has no stone stairs to access it from outside (fig. ). Below the entrance, a large protruding rock slops upward. The cave may, originally, have been reached by wooden stairs, or perhaps merely by a ladder. Today it is only accessible from cave 58 through a door that was broken into wall d (which is simultaneously wall f of cave 58, fig. ), when pigs were kept in the cave in the 1960s.

list.item: Wall a: height 128 cm, width 36–46 cm.
Wall d: height at the front 239 cm, at the rear 252 cm, width 251 cm.
Wall e: height at midpoint 249 cm; width 257 cm.
Wall f: height at the front 250 cm, at the rear 247 cm, width 279 cm.
Width of opening 208 cm.

figure.head: 20世紀鑿出的58號窟與59號窟之間的門道。2010年拍攝
Twentieth century door between cave 58 and cave 59; photograph taken in 2010

div.head: Changes
div.p: The most dramatic change is the door that was broken through from cave 58 (fig. ). Its threshold is 32 cm and the lintel 210 cm above the floor. At the foot, the door opening is 56 cm wide, and at the top 58 cm. A groove was cut into the floor near the entrance to take fluids out of the cave.

div.head: Layout
div.p: The polished area measures 126 x 36–46 cm. The field of script measures 113.5 x 33 cm, its lower edge is 13 cm above the floor of the cave. The inscription has very slightly cut into the southern side of the adjacent niche 61 (fig. ), and must therefore be later.

row.cell: in columns 1–14; lowest row not used
first ten columns 21 cm; thereafter tighter
first ten columns 1.0 cm; thereafter tighter

div.head: State of Preservation
div.p: Column 16 is extensively erased in its lower part; a black patina covers the upper right corner of the field of script.

row.cell: 佛說報父母恩重經; refers to the beginning of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents (Fo shuo bao fumu enzhong jing 佛説報父母恩重經).
報父母恩重經; refers to the end of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents; the characters fo shuo 佛說 have here been omitted from the title.

figure.head: 緊鄰59號窟a壁的61號龕。2010年拍攝
Niche 61, with wall a of cave 59 to the left; photograph taken in 2010

div.head: Layout

list.item: Frame with ornamental borders.

div.p: Upper border: height 5–17 cm, two apsaras emerge from clouds, the right one flies to the left, the left one swings down in a turn from above, his head points toward the right.
Northern border: width 6–7 cm, a running scroll including flowers and Buddha figures.
Southern border: width 16–17 cm, on account of the door opening, only the lower and upper parts of a running scroll are extant.
Lower border: height 20 cm, cartouches in seven kun-shaped openings (kunmen 壼門), filled with animals.

list.item: The polished area coincides with the trapezoidal field of script; 192 cm high at the front and 216 cm at the rear, but only 21 cm of height is left above the lintel of the twentieth century door. The field is 226 cm wide, but is cut to 186 cm in its lower part by the door opening. The upper edge of the field rises in steps toward the rear: columns 29–68 are higher than the first 28 columns by one row; the field rises by another row in column 69, by two rows in columns 70–76, and by another row in columns 77 to the last column 107; the lower edge is mainly horizontal, it ends 29 cm above the floor in the front, and 21 cm at the rear.
On stylistic grounds, the field of script can also be divided into four sections:

div.p: columns 1–28;
columns 29–68;
columns 69–76;
column 76 (from the title of scroll 2)–107.

row.cell: 1–28, thereafter faint traces of vertical lines
columns 1–28: 21.6 cm; columns 29–68: 20.7 cm; columns 69–76: 20.5 cm; columns 76–107: 21.2 cm
columns 1–28: ca. 0.1–1.1 cm; columns 29–68: ca. 0.2–1.2 cm; columns 69–76: 0.6–1.2 cm; column 76–107: 0.1–1.0 cm

columns 1–28: 0.4–1.1 cm; columns 29–68: ca. 0.2 cm; columns 69–76: 0.1–0.9 cm, the tightest and also the most irregular of the four sections; has running script (xingshu) elements, ends with scroll 1 in column 76; in columns 76–107; beginning with the title of scroll 2, the layout becomes more regular again, 0.1–0.5 cm

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: Generally good. The surface is not even; near the entrance, 149–163 cm above the floor, a rectangular man-made hole with an extension on the left, cuts into the ornamental border and obliterates parts of columns 1–9.

A row of small natural horizontal crevices, 148–151 cm above the floor, extends from columns 45–71; they existed already before the carving began; the carvers took them into account. Another row of smaller crevices, ca. 140 cm above the floor, extends from columns 67–89.

A flaked zone extends across the entire wall, 46–53 cm above the floor at the front, and 25–47 cm at the rear. In the center of the wall, a black patina covers a zone ca. 32–102 cm above the floor, beginning ca. 80 cm from the entrance and reaching to the door. Ink graffiti are seen in an area ca. 100–164 cm from the entrance and ca. 100–205 cm above the floor. The last 19 columns have been cut away by the door. Only one fragment survived.
A crack descends from the rear part of the ceiling, ending above the apsaras at the front.

row.cell: 大般涅槃經壽命品第一; refers to the beginning of part 1 in chapter 1, entitled Shouming pin 壽命品 in scroll 1, of the Nirvana Sutra.
大般涅槃經卷第一; refers to the end of scroll 1, coinciding with the end of part 1 of chapter 1, entitled Shouming pin , of the Nirvana Sutra.

大般涅槃經壽命品之二; followed by a small character 2, er 二; refers to the beginning of part 2 of chapter 1, entitled Shouming pin, coinciding with the beginning of scroll 2 of the Nirvana Sutra. The small character 二 refers to the beginning of scroll 2 of the Nirvana Sutra.
衆生皆; the last three characters on wall d, the upper part of zhong 衆 is damaged.

div.head: Layout

list.item: Frame with ornamental borders.

div.p: Upper border: height 15.5–18 cm, two apsaras carrying bowls emerge from clouds and fly toward a symmetrical flower bouquet in the center.
West side: width 10 cm, a running scroll including flowers and Buddha figures.
East side: width 14 cm, a running scroll including flowers and Buddha figures.
Below: height 20 cm, cartouches in six kun-shaped openings, filled with animals and a musician.

list.item: The polished area extends 2.0–2.5 cm beyond the ornamental border on the west and ca. 3.5–8.5 cm in the east.

The rectangular field of script within the borders is 226 cm wide; the height at the west is 207 cm, at the east 208 cm; the upper edge is approximately horizontal. The distance from the floor is 25 cm on the west and 20 cm on the east; the lower edge is irregular.

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: Generally good. The surface is fairly even. Movements in the cliff have caused a triangular section in the upper left corner to push forward. It is separated from the main area of the wall by a crack descending from the rear part of the ceiling, from which horizontal layers have fallen. The crack has been filled in by cement and continues into the ornamental border, where it peters out ca. 88 cm above the floor. Another vertical crack runs down all the way in the corner between walls e and f. It has also been filled by cement. This one continues diagonally into the floor. These cracks are the result of forces within the mountain and are connected to a crack in the adjacent cave 66. There it runs through the entire wall d, over the ceiling into wall f and continues on the floor.

A horizontal crack with a row of clay galls, ca. 128–148 cm above ground, begins at the corner with wall d and reaches ca. 157 cm into the wall, creating several slits from columns 1–72; a rectangular man-made hole, 78–87 cm above the floor, damages columns 31–34; a rectangular man-made hole, 145–156 cm above the floor, obliterates parts of columns 30–35.
On the left a black surface covers a zone, ca. 14–145 cm, above the floor.

row.cell: faint traces of vertical lines in the left area
悉悦可纯陁……; continuation from wall d.
大般涅槃經卷第二; refers to the end of scroll 2, coinciding with the end of part 2 of chapter 1, entitled Shouming pin , of the Nirvana Sutra.
大般涅槃經卷第三; refers to the beginning of scroll 3, coinciding with the beginning of part 3, the last part of chapter 1, entitled Shouming pin , of the Nirvana Sutra.
大般涅槃經金剛身品第二; refers to the beginning of chapter 2, entitled Jin’gangshen pin 金剛身品, in scroll 3 of the Nirvana Sutra.
然後乃扵娑羅; the last characters on wall e.

div.head: Layout

list.item: Frame with ornamental borders.

div.p: Upper border: height 18 cm at the rear, 10 cm at the front, two apsaras, the right one carrying a bowl, fly from clouds toward a symmetrical flower bouquet in the center.
Southern border: width 8 cm, a running scroll including flowers and Buddha figures.
Northern border: almost completely destroyed.
Below: height 20 cm, cartouches in six kun-shaped openings remaining, largely eroded.

list.item: The polished area extends 3.5–5.0 cm beyond the ornamental border at the rear.

The field of script is an approximate parallelogram. While the lower edge rises in steps towards the rear, the upper edge rises continuously. The width is 249 cm; the height at the front is 202 cm, at the rear 213 cm; the distance from the floor at the front is 25 cm, at the rear 18 cm. The lower edge is irregular.

row.cell: remnants of vertical lines until column 73
ca. 0.1–0.6 cm until column 73; thereafter ca. 0.4–0.6 cm
ca. 0.0–0.3 cm until column 73; thereafter 0.2–0.5 cm

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: Generally good. The surface is not even; a horizontal crack forms, about 152–159 cm above the floor, a row of several clay galls in columns 12–93. A small man-made hole, 82–87 cm above the floor, obliterates parts of columns 3–5. An incision for a door frame, 157 x 8 cm, extends from the floor into columns 93–96 (fig. ). A round hole, 161.5–171 cm above the floor, which corresponds to a similar hole in wall d, obliterates parts of columns 92–98; another round hole, 158–161 cm above the floor, cuts into columns 110–111. Another man-made hole, 231–238 cm from the floor, above columns 99–103, does not affect the carved area.
Areas between the left side of the incision for the door frame and the entrance have flaked off.

row.cell: 雙樹入般涅槃……; continuation from wall e

大般涅槃經卷第三; refers to scroll 3 in the Nirvana Sutra, where chapter 2, entitled Jin’gangshen pin ends.In scroll 3 of the sutra now follows chapter 3, entitled Mingzi gongde pin 名字功德品. This chapter, however, is not engraved. Instead, the carving immediately continues with chapter 4 in scroll 4.

大般涅槃經如来性品第四; refers to the beginning of scroll 4, coinciding with the beginning of part 1 of chapter 4, entitled Rulaixing pin
如來性品, of the Nirvana Sutra.
大般涅槃經卷第四; refers to the end of scroll 4 of the Nirvana Sutra, coinciding with the end of part 1 in chapter 4, entitled Rulaixing pin 如來性品.

大乘大集地藏十輪經卷第三; refers to scroll 3 in the Great Vehicle Great Collection Sutra on the Earth-Store Bodhisattva and the Ten Wheels (Dasheng daji Dizang shi lun jing
大乘大集地藏十輪經; hereafter: Sutra on the Ten Wheels), which is the first source text of the excerpt which follow after the next title.

佛說禪法略出: below referred to by its more common title Scripture on the Secret Essentials of Meditation (Chan miyao jing 禪秘要經),For the varying titles of the text see above, . which is the second source text for excerpts that follow below.
佛言大梵有十種……; refers to the beginning of the excerpt from the Sutra on the Ten Wheels.
space after 大苦and before 尒時世尊.
智者應供養; the last characters of the excerpt from the Sutra on the Ten Wheels.
禪秘要經一部三卷; refers to the Scripture on the Secret Essentials of Meditation, one text in three scrolls.
已上出十輪經第三卷; refers to the end of the excerpts from scroll 3 of the Sutra on the Ten Wheels.
第二卷; refers to scroll 2 of the Scripture on the Secret Essentials of Meditation, from which the following twelve excerpts are culled.
略出初學坐禪法流通末代得者頂戴受持……; statement of intent, by whom and how these text should be used.
又今我身内自有四; space before 又. Beginning of the third excerpt from the Scripture on Secret Essentials of Meditation.
space after 得生天上and before 若復有人.
千二百五十比丘皆. Beginning of the sixth excerpt from the Scripture on Secret Essentials of Meditation.
45 characters omitted between 貪淫多者 and 復當繫念住意.
202 characters omitted between 往反至十 and 或單或複.
或單或複修長短随意. Beginning of the eighth excerpt from the Scripture on Secret Essentials of Meditation.
歡喜奉行; end of the eleventh excerpt from the Scripture on Secret Essentials of Meditation.

禪法秘要第二卷; space before 禪. Refers to scroll 2 of the Secret Essential Methods of the Meditation (above called Scripture on the Secret Essentials of Meditation 禪秘要經), from which the excerpts above and below were culled.

如是我聞; refers to the beginning of the twelfth excerpt from scroll 2 of the Scripture on the Secret Essentials of Meditation, coinciding with the beginning of the third of a total of four individual sutras of which the Scripture on the Secret Essentials of Meditation is composed.
space after 敷尼師檀結跏趺坐 and before 尔時眾中有一比丘名.
space after 說止觀法 and before 佛告槃直迦汝從今日.
space after 端坐繫心 and before 佛告槃直迦汝今當.
亦如上說; refers to the end of the twelfth excerpt from scroll 2 of the Scripture on the Secret Essentials of Meditation.

禪經卷第三; refers to scroll 3 of the Meditation Scripture 禪經 (above called Scripture on the Secret Essentials of Meditation
禪秘要經), from which the three excerpts below were culled.
佛告阿難; space before 佛. Refers to the beginning of the first excerpt from scroll 3 of the Meditation Scripture.
space after 必墮惡道犯大重罪 and before 若有比丘尼妖冶邪媚.
space after 必墮惡道犯大重罪 and before 若有比丘比丘尼實不見白骨.
［不可具說］, damaged; refers to the end of the first excerpt from scroll 3 of the Meditation Scripture.
［佛告阿難］, damaged; refers to the beginning of the second excerpt from scroll 3 of the Meditation Scripture.
悟觧脱道; refers to the end of the second excerpt from scroll 3 of the Meditation Scripture.
若有比丘比……; space before 若. Refers to the beginning of the third excerpt from scroll 3 of the Meditation Scripture.

［佛］說禪祕要經; refers to the end of all excerpts from the Scripture on the Secret Essentials of Meditation Spoken by the Buddha: twelve excerpts have been taken from scroll 2, and three excerpts from scroll 3.
開元廿三年二月十五日長江縣李涉敬造供養; the colophon of 735 refers to the excerpts on meditation from the preceding two texts.
佛說報父母恩重經; refers to the beginning of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents (Fo shuo bao fumu enzhong jing 佛説報父母恩重經).
［報父母恩重經］; refers to the end of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents (Fo shuo bao fumu enzhong jing 佛説報父母恩重經).
last column on wall f: ……年六月卄六日清信女滿□供［養］ ; Colophon at the end of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents, refers only to this sutra.

note.p: The authors give titles of texts, and compare the apsaras to those in niche 7 in Huangze Monastery 皇澤寺, Guangyuan 廣元, and in Longmen 龍門 and Gongxian 鞏縣.

Peng gives measurements of the cave and computes its volume as 16.302 cubic meters. He identifies all scrolls and chapters of the Nirvana Sutra carved on walls d, e, and f. He indicates the passage taken from scroll 3 of the Great Vehicle Great Collection Sutra on the Earth-Store Bodhisattva and the Ten Wheels (Dasheng daji Dizang shi lun jing 大乘大集地藏十輪經), but lists the second title of this passage, Excerpts from the Meditation Methods Spoken by the Buddha (Fo shuo chanfa lüechu 佛説禪法略出), as if it was an independent scripture. He also lists the Scripture on the Secret Essentials of Meditation (Chan miyao jing 禪秘要經) on wall f, and both occurrences of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents (Fo shuo bao fumu enzhong jing 佛説報父母恩重經) on walls a and f. In addition, he transcribes the two colophons in the cave, reading the names of donors as Li Sha 李沙 and Man Yue 滿月.
Cao mentions both colophons carved in the cave, one by Li Sha 李沙 of 735, and one by the pious laywoman Man [. . .] 滿□.

Hu Wenhe sets out the measurements of the three dimensions of the cave (as does 6–7), but he does not compute its volume. Hu adopts the same locations of the texts as given by Peng Jiasheng, omitting, however, the Sutra on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents 佛説報父母恩重經 on wall a. He also gives a transcription of the two colophons on wall f. In addition, Hu provides a short outline of the textual history of the Sutra on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents. He reads the name of the donors as Li She 李涉 and Man [. . .] 滿□.

Deng Zhijin says that all walls in this cave are covered with texts, but he only enumerates the titles of chapters and scrolls of the texts carved on wall f. He also provides transcriptions of the colophons on wall f, and reads the names of the donors as Li Sha 李沙 and Man Xiang 滿相.
Hu Wenhe basically repeats the information given in 7, 9.

Sun Xiushen travelled to Wofoyuan in 1995, when he learned about the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents carved on wall f in cave 59. Before transcribing this text, he first gives an introduction into the different text editions:
The text of the Jia Edition 甲本 contains references to Chinese exemplars of filial piety, which led to it being marked as a dubious scripture in the Kaiyuan Era Catalog of Śākyamuni’s Teachings.
The Yi Edition 乙本, to which most of the surviving Dunhuang manuscripts belong, does not contain the Chinese exemplars of filial piety. It is published in volume 85 of the modern Taishō edition as T#2887.
The Bing Edition 丙本, which also does not contain the exemplars of filial piety, provides numbers for the particular kindnesses of parents from one to ten, and also short titles for each of them. It further mentions punishments received in hell for unfilial behaviour of sons and daughters.
The Ding Edition 丁本 is only found on a silk scroll discovered in the hidden library in cave 17 in Dunhuang; it is held to be a revision of the Yi Edition with an enlarged title, Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents, although it was very much abbreviated in content. This was made possibly to adapt the layout of the silk scroll. For each of these four editions, Sun Xiushen provides one recension in full text. He also mentions the Go­ryeo Edition 高麗本, which was first identified by Makita Tairyō 牧田諦亮. Then Sun Xiushen provides a transcription of the text carved on wall f in cave 59, which he collates with the texts of the Jia and the Yi Editions. Adopting the date of 735 for the text in cave 59 as proposed in 291, he thus considers it the earliest surviving recension, which he places as a link between the Jia and the Yi Editions.

Sun Xiushen does not mention the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents that was carved on wall a of cave 59, although it is much better preserved than the text on wall f.

The authors give measurements, list numbers of columns on each wall, compute numbers of characters, identify all sutras and chapters, make identifications of the motives in the ornamental borders, and date the cave to the Tang period. They read the names of the donors as Li Sha 李沙 and Man Xiang 滿相.

Reverend Arai Keiyō 新井慧誉 (1939–2007), a Shingon 真言 monk, Abbot of Jutokuji 寿徳寺, received his PhD from Tokyo University with a thesis on the Sutra on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents (Bao fumu enzhong jing 報父母恩重經). He visited the site in 1997 and photographed, documented and studied the two texts versions carved in cave 59 in detail. By collating the carved text versions with the Old Edition 古本 of the same text, he reconstructed the so-called Bao Edition 報本. It adds the character bao
報 (“repaying”) in front of the title of the Old Edition, Sutra on the Profound Kindness of Parents (Fumu en­zhong jing 父母恩重經).
Cheng A-Tsai 鄭阿財 lists all 60 Dunhuang manuscripts of the Sutra on the Profound Kindness of Parents (Fumu enzhong jing 父母恩重經) known to him, and he divides them into four editions:

list.item: The Jia Edition 甲本, also called the Ding Lan Edition 丁蘭本, in ca. 978 characters. It contains references to three Chinese exemplars of filial piety, one of them being Ding Lan. This edition matches the one mentioned in the Kaiyuan Era Catalog of Śākyamuni’s Teachings (Kaiyuan shijiao lu 開元釋教録).

The Yi Edition 乙本, also called the Old Edition 古本, in ca. 875 characters. It is close to the Jia Edition, but it omits the text passage in 103 characters mentioning the three Chinese exemplars of filial piety.
The Bing Edition 丙本, which is close to the Yi Edition, but contains a special recommendation to follow the ullambana (yulan pen 盂蘭盆) observances.

The enlarged Ding Edition 丁本, which contains an enumeration of the ten particular kind actions of parents (fumu shi ende 父母十恩德), as well as punishments for unfilial behaviour in 18 hells. This edition is related to popular preaching texts (jiangjing wen 講經文) and is often illustrated in transformation tableaux (jiangbian tu 經變圖).

note.p: For each of these text traditions, Cheng lists surviving Dunhuang manuscripts and quotes the sutra text. When talking about stone versions of the Sutra on the Profound Kindness of Parents, Cheng mentions the text carved on wall f in cave 59 in the Grove of the Reclining Buddha. Based on Hu Wenhe’s research, who was not aware of the better preserved text on wall a, Cheng emphasizes that the text in concern is the earliest version of the sutra, and grouped it to the Yi Edition. The article is reprinted in 1–25.

The editors of the Chengdu Municipal Institute of Cultural Relics and Archaeology provide detailed descriptions of the architecture of the cave, including measurements of all walls. The ornaments in relief are described in great detail. In their table, the editors give the column numbers for each scroll, references to the texts in the Taishō
edition, and they transcribe the colophons.

Cao and Yu give measurements of the walls, their surface areas, and based on rubbings the number of extant characters, wall d: 9,844; wall e: 12,971; wall f: 13,552. They identify the scrolls and chapters of the Nirvana Sutra carved in this cave. They also mention the titles of The Great Vehicle Great Collection Sutra on the Earth-Store Bodhisattva and the Ten Wheels, scroll 3 大乘大集地藏十輪經卷第三, and the Meditation Scripture, scroll 3 禪經卷第三, but they omit the two carvings of the Sutra on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents in this cave. Finally, they provide transcriptions of the two colophons on wall f.

Lee includes cave 59 in her cluster III, which corresponds to section C. She names all sutras carved in this cave, omitting the Sutra on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents carved on the outside wall a. She thinks that the initial intention was to carve the Nirvana Sutra not in its entirety, but only in selection. Cave 59 and cave 66 were designed primarily for this sutra, while caves 46 and 51 contain additional sections that may have been added as an afterthought. Lee says that the Nirvana Sutra was confined to her clusters III and IV, not noting that another passage from this sutra was also carved in cave 83 on wall d.

Lee also suggests that the two texts on meditation in cave 59 show connections with the Three Levels Teachings. Pointing at the sutra carvings in Jinchuanwan, Shaanxi, she notes that, at both sites, the Lotus Sutra, the Diamond Sutra, and the Sutra on the Ten Wheels were carved, albeit the latter sutra was carved in its older Northern Liang version (T#410) at Jinchuanwan, and in the revised translation by Xuanzang (T#411) at the Grove.

The authors give a detailed description of the location and architecture of the cave, as well as of the ornaments in relief. The position of the texts of the sutra scrolls and damaged areas are indicated in measured drawings of the walls. The colophons are transcribed. The engraved sutra texts are also completely transcribed, albeit without punctuation; differences with the Taishō
edition are indicated.

Deng Xingliang gives a general description of the cave and of its ornamental borders, as well as some measurements. He provides a transcription of the colophon of 735, which follows in column 105 after the excerpts from the Scripture on the Secret Essentials of Meditation, reading the name of the donor as Li Sha 李沙. Because of the spatial proximity of this colophon to the second colophon on the same wall f, in column 113, following the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents, by the laywomen, whose name Deng reads as Man Xiang 滿相, he proposes that this second, damaged colophon was carved in the same year (735) as the one by Li Sha.

Deng further lists all chapters and scrolls of the Nirvana Sutra carved on walls d, e, and f, indicating the numbers of the column in which they begin and end, and he also quotes short text passages at the beginning and end of each such chapter or scroll. In a table, he compares the divisions into chapters and scrolls of the text carved in the Grove with the divisions in the texts of the Northern and Southern editions of the Nirvana Sutra. He concludes that the stone edition is an excerpt from the Northern edition, comprising texts from scrolls 1–14, in which some scroll and chapter divisions were newly arranged.

Deng further notes that the texts of The Great Vehicle Great Collection Sutra on the Earth-Store Bodhisattva and the Ten Wheels and the Scripture on the Secret Essentials of Meditation carved on wall f both consist of excerpts. He transcribes all carved titles of these two texts, indicating their sequence, and quoting the beginning and end of the text following each title; however, he neither gives a detailed analysis of the number of excerpts, nor of the omitted text parts.

Turning to the last text on wall f, the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents in nine columns, Deng first transcribes the colophon following it. He also mentions the same sutra on wall a in 16 columns, which was discovered and first transcribed by Arai Keiyo 新井慧誉. In his discussion of the various editions of this sutra, Deng adopts the four main lineages as defined by Cheng A-tsai 鄭阿財 (30–33).

Deng also provides transcriptions of the texts on walls a and f, basing himself on the work done by Arai Keiyo (). Arai’s reconstruction of the Wofoyuan edition, which Arai calls Bao Edition 報本, supplements missing characters from the Old Edition古本. Deng suggests that it would have been better to supplement the missing parts from the Bing Edition. In conclusion, Deng argues that the texts carved at the Grove constitute an edition on their own, for three reasons:

list.item: Although the carved texts are close to the Yi and Bing Editions, the missing text in the lower part of column 15 on wall a cannot be amended by the passage in either one of these editions, because Deng assumes that there is not enough space.

item.p:
Although the carved texts belong to the larger group of Sutras on the Profound Kindness of Parents
父母恩重經, the formulation of their title, Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents 佛説報父母恩重經, is unique. A scripture with this exact title is not mentioned in any Buddhist catalog. The texts versions at the Grove are very early recensions, which must have been circulating before 735, the date of the colophon in cave 59. The creation of the text must therefore date to before the kaiyuan 開元 era (713–741).

A Daoist scripture, entitled Sutra Spoken by the Most High Elder Lord on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents 太上老君説報父母恩重經, was identified by Akizuki Kan’ei 秋月觀暎 as the prototype for the Buddhist Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on Repaying the Profound Kindness of Parents (). Because of the rather early date of the two texts at the Grove, Deng proposes that it was rather the Buddhist sutra that was copied by the Daoists, and not vice versa.

list.item: Some phrases and words of the text at the Grove differ from those in other editions, reflecting changes during the process of transmission.

note.p: In a table listing such changes, Deng juxtaposes the text of the Wofoyuan Edition with those of the Jia, Yi, and Bing Editions, as well as with a later Song version.
